{{Spoiler|note=Contains spoilers for all Time Beacon information in [[Time Rift]].}}

{{WIP}}

=Time Beacon=
The Time Beacon is a unique element to the [[Time Rift]]. It contains your Temporal Drifters and Anecdotes.

You unlock the Time Beacon when you [[Time_Rift_Apostles#Dawie_Jones|Defeat 4th Apostle (Dawie Jones - AD 1556 - Berfast)]] and unlock [[Time_Beacon#St._Trinidad|St. Trinidad]] and [[Time_Beacon#Soleil_Royal|Soleil Royal]].

=Temporal Drifters=
Drifters are recruited as you defeat apostles in the Time Rift. Each Drifter will have a Rift exploration bonus, 5 skills for Anecdote trials, and Talents. When a drifter is recruited, they are at level 1 with 1 skill unlocked. Drifters are leveled up by spending Time Fruit and additional items collected while exploring the rift. Skills are learned by having Drifters attend the skill Academies in the Time Beacon. Skills are leveled by spending Time Worms.

=Anecdotes=
Anecdotes are a series of challenges in the Time Beacon in the Time Rift. When the Time Beacon is unlocked, the only Anecdote that is available is Temporal Barrier - 01. When you defeat an Anecdote you gain rewards and unlock further challenges.

==Academy==
Academy events teach select Temporal Drifters certain skills. It takes 12 Hours for a Temporal Drifter to learn a skill. Each Academy can be used multiple times to teach multiple Temporal Drifters. Temporal Drifters have a preset list of skills they can learn and a particular order they have to learn them in. But only one Drifter can be taught at an academy at a time and a Drifter can only be learning one skill at a time. After a Drifter finishes learning a skill, depending on their skill set, they can then go back to the same Academy or a different Academy if they still have open skills. If the Academy is then free, a different student can attend.

===Band Academy===
The Band Academy is set in AD 1875 and is unlocked by defeating the Paris Opera House Band event. It teaches the following Band skills.

'''Potential Students:''' [[Time_Beacon#Athena|Athena]], [[Time_Beacon#Dracula|Dracula]], [[Time_Beacon#Idol_Boy|Idol Boy]], [[Time_Beacon#Koryeon_Commissioner|Koryeon Commissioner]], [[Time_Beacon#Madam_Huang_EX|Madam Huang EX]]
{| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"
!Drifter
!Order Number
!Skill Name
!Skill Effect
|-
|[[Time_Beacon#Athena|Athena]]||1st||Athena's Delight||When performing, cast Athena's Delight by +55%:<br>Elicits cheers from the audience<br>Skill Type: Active Skill (Band)
|-

|-

|-

|-
|[[Time_Beacon#Idol_Boy|Idol Boy]]||1st||Passionate Samba||When performing Remix:<br>> Band Talent Effect +5%<br>Skill Type: Passive Skill (Band)
|-

==Band==
Band events are similar to the minigame from the [[EDM Festival]] event. You create a band of five Temporal Drifters and perform a song for the audience.

==Dance==
Dance events work similar to the [[Dancing Gala]] minigame from [[Hellas]]. You pick a team of Temporal Drifters to compete. Your Temporal Drifters will face off, one by one, against the opposing team. Whoever has a higher score will win their match. You will need to win a majority of matches overall to win the challenge.

The order of your Temporal Drifters will need to match up with the order of your opponents. When you assign a Temporal Drifter, their opponent will flash red above. The list of opponents below is in the order you will face them and their score is next to their name.

!Time
!Enemy List
!Recommended Dance

!Reward
!Unlocks

|}

==Defense==
Defense events are similar to the [[Kemet Pyramids]] minigame from [[Kemet]]. You select three Temporal Drifters to hold the wall from an invading army.

==Dharma==
Dharma encounters work similarly to the [[Journey to the West]] minigame from [[Bharata]]. You pick a team of four of your Temporal Drifters and use their skills to fight your opponents.

==Duels==
Duels work similar to regular fights in the rest of the game. Your snail has a power stat given to it as do all of your Temporal Drifters.

===Stand Alone Duels===
After finishing an Anecdote, sometimes there are stand alone duels with a final boss.

===Time Keepers===
The Time Keepers are a string of fights that are in some ways gate keepers to new Anecdotes in the Time Beacon. They get progressively harder as you go and unlock new encounters. Also, defeating certain amounts of Time Keepers unlocks Time Keeper Packs in the [[Museum#Museum_Shop|Museum Shop]].

=== Progressive Duels ===
Some encounters have multiple levels to them that each have a separate Duel. Similar to how "towers" work in the Domains.

==== Four Holy Pagoda ====
Four Holy Pagoda in AD 25 is unlocked by defeating the Dharma event Mayhem Maker.

The Four Holy Ones' trials lie in the pagoda.

==Ninjutsu==
Ninjutsu encounters work similarly to the [[Journey to the West]] minigame from [[Bharata]]. You pick a team of three of your Temporal Drifters and use their skills to fight a single opponent.

!Time
!Enemy Name
!Recommended Ninjutsu
!Weakness
!Reward
!Unlocks

==Riding==
Riding events are similar to the minigame from the [[Golden Week]] event. In it, you race three Temporal Drifters versus a team of three racers. Each of your Temporal Drifters races against a separate member of the opposition team.

!Time
!Enemy Name
!Recommended Riding

!Reward

==Seafaring==
Seafaring events work similar to the [[Mysterious Sea]] minigame from [[Britland]]. You select a crew of four Temporal Drifters and set sail with them against a map. The maps are progressive and you can come back to them in the same run in the Time Rift or across multiple runs just like in Britland.

!Event Name
!Time
!Enemy Name
!Recommended Seafaring
!Unlocks

==Siege==
Siege events are like super Fights. Your entire team of Temporal Drifters plows through lots of minions and then squares off against the Chief in a normal Fight. Once your Temporal Drifters defeat the Chief and their army, the Chief will challenge the snail to a duel.

{| class="wikitable"
!Event Name
!Time
!Recommended Siege
!Defenders
!Siege Reward
!Unlocks
